titleStmt.title: Letter from a Local Government Board inspector to C. J. Hanrette, 11 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Local Government Board Inspector

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from a Local Government Board (LGB) inspector to C. J. Hanrette requesting information on the minimum wage for various trades in Ireland. The author informs that the information is required in the preparation of a report that is being drawn up.C.J. Hanrette was an employee at the Labour Exchange, Dublin.

correspAction.placeName: Divisional Office, Labour Exchange, Lord Edward Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Local Government Board, Dublin. 11th March 1916. Dear Mr. Hanrette, In connection with a report I am preparing I have to
ascertain what trades in Ireland a minimum wage has been fixed
for. I would be very much obliged if you couldlet me have
this information naming the minimum fixed in each case. With kind regards,
Yours truly, Local Govt. Inspector. C.J Hanrette, Esq., Divisional Office, Labour Exchange, Lord Edward St., Dublin.
